claim_boundary: The route proves field inventory, projections, and disposition only; behavior implementation and test closure require downstream current evidence.

closure_rules.scope: current target revision and declared route only

forbidden_shortcuts.summary: Do not create a SkillGuard-owned alternate execution route.
Do not convert configured booleans, generated JSON, or progress into native pass evidence.
Do not skip required stages, checks, receipts, or blockers.

integration_claim_boundary: SkillGuard gates the target-native FlowGuard route; it does not execute a parallel controller or replace native model/test evidence.

native_check_inventory.summary: Run field lifecycle, projection, and disposition tests.

native_route_bindings.source: FlowGuard canonical route registry and target contract source

not_parallel_route_proof.evidence_source: Canonical route registry, contract source, and may_define_* false gates
not_parallel_route_proof.summary: SkillGuard only validates the FlowGuard-owned route and cannot define an alternate successful execution path.

phases.summary: Define the field boundary, change intent, compatibility policy, and claim scope.
Create parent groups and one leaf row for every discovered field with owner/readers/writers/lifecycle.
Project behavior-bearing fields, hand all ordinary-UI-reader fields to UI content admission, and route invalid/old-field finite cases to ContractExhaustionMesh.
Run field lifecycle tests and reject missing rows, UI-reader handoff gaps, projection gaps, duplicate owners, or unknown dispositions.
Return projections, canonical cases, owner maps, dispositions, and typed downstream evidence obligations.

quality_floors.summary: Prompt prose, progress, or generated records cannot replace native evidence.
Missing, stale, skipped, scoped, progress-only, or pass-with-gaps evidence cannot support broad closure.

required_evidence.source: FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact
FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact
FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact
FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact
FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact

route_inventory.summary: Account all discovered leaf fields, project behavior-bearing fields, and close replacement/compatibility disposition.

do_not_use_when: No field-like surface is in scope or the question is downstream behavior/test proof rather than field ownership and disposition.
routes.summary: Account all discovered leaf fields, project behavior-bearing fields, and close replacement/compatibility disposition.

runtime_lock_policy.applies_when: The flowguard-field-lifecycle-mesh skill is selected or delegated.
runtime_lock_policy.summary: The target-local contract, route, stage order, checks, evidence, and coverage matrix gate closure.

source_requirements.summary: Behavior-bearing fields require projections, single owners/readers/writers, and canonical invalid/old cases.
Every discovered field requires a leaf row or a narrower documented discovery boundary.
Old, replaced, deprecated, alias, or fallback fields require a concrete disposition before broad confidence.
Every field whose reader reaches an ordinary UI boundary hands off to UI content admission regardless of source role; fields with no ordinary-UI reader remain internally accounted.
Accept a bounded field inventory; create leaf rows and behavior projections; block missing fields or unknown old-field disposition; hand canonical malformed cases, owners, obligations, and.
Route id: field_lifecycle_mesh; role: public_owner; native owner: field_lifecycle_mesh. This standalone FlowGuard satellite skill owns field boundaries and projections, not downstream behav.
Use for schema/payload/config/prompt/persisted/UI/runtime fields, migrations, aliases, defaults, fallbacks, or field-rooted model misses.
The target section 'Use When' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
Default replacement requires delete, block, migrate, delegate, repair, explicit preserve, or scoped reason; unknown disposition blocks full confidence.
The target section 'Hard Gates' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
Return evidence, failures, blockers, skipped_checks, residual_risk, claim_boundary, and typed_next_actions, plus leaf rows, projections, owners, and dispositions.
Verify the real FlowGuard check engine and AGENTS.md managed record; never create a fake mini-framework.
The target skill description must be represented in the contract: Use when a change adds, removes, renames, migrates, replaces, externalizes, preserves, or audits fields, schema keys, confi.
The target skill entrypoint and hard gates must be represented by the runtime contract.
Edit .skillguard/contract-source.json, then regenerate derived contracts; SkillGuard validates field accounting and cannot fabricate behavior or compatibility evidence.
Declared hard gates and required workflow steps must be represented by checks and closure blockers.
Do not put every field in the high-level model, treat inventory as behavior proof, or replace alignment/testing; return missing behavior models to model-first-function-flow.
The target section 'Do Not Use When' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
The target section 'Output Requirements' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
Add FieldProjection rows only for behavior-bearing fields and route invalid/old cases to ContractExhaustionMesh.
Behavior claims still require current obligations, owner code contracts, tests, freshness, and template harvest closure where the model deepens.
Close replacement disposition and send owners, readers/writers, projections, cases, and gaps downstream.
Define the field boundary, parent groups, and every discovered FieldLifecycleRow.
The target section 'Required Workflow' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
